item.title: 'Frenzy' expected as Prince Harry mulls family trip to UK for first time in years
item.description: Prince Harry says he is still trying to bring his family to Britain next week, despite concerns of a "frenzy" as paparazzi attempt to snap rare photographs of his children.

item.title: Charges dropped against two teens who allegedly abducted 17yo girl outside school
item.description: Victoria Police have dropped charges against two males who were arrested and charged with the abduction of a teenage girl outside a Melbourne school.

item.title: Nervous wait for NSW poultry industry after suspected positive H5 bird flu case
item.description: Nervous times ahead for the NSW poultry industry, with one producer believing it is "only a matter of time" before commercial operations are affected by H5 bird flu.

item.title: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart renews bid for helipad at Hancock Prospecting HQ
item.description: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart wants to be able to land a helicopter on the roof of her corporate HQ, and says the noise experienced by nearby residents would be similar to using a coffee machine or blender.

item.title: Adelaide music festival Harvest Rock won't take place in 2026, promoter confirms
item.description: Promoter Secret Sounds has announced that the SA government-backed event won't be going ahead this October.

item.title: DarkLab ditches Transformer artwork in Huon Valley due to escalating costs
item.description: The group behind Tasmania's Dark Mofo festival has scrapped plans to develop a "destination public artwork" in the state's south-east due to escalating costs.

item.title: Souths captain Cody Walker apologises after kicking opponent
item.description: South Sydney captain Cody Walker has said he let his teammates down after being sin-binned on Friday night for kicking an opposition player.

item.title: Australia raises concerns as China strengthens control over maritime traffic east of Taiwan
item.description: Australia raises concerns about China's latest activities near Taiwan "directly to Chinese officials" as tensions simmer in the region.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Perth paediatrician Chia Liang Saw unknowingly communicated with self-described paedophile vigilante, court told
item.description: A Perth paediatrician alleged to have sexually assaulted child patients was unknowingly communicating online with a self-described paedophile vigilante, a court has heard.

item.title: Social media backlash after live music venue includes AI act in emerging artist showcase
item.description: Local musician Aidan Sammut says he was disgusted after a live music event aimed at showcasing local artists included an artist who used AI-generated content.

item.title: Video shows Indonesian military recovering body of US pilot killed in Papua
item.description: A video shows an operation by the Indonesian military to recover the body of an American pilot, shot dead by Papuan separatists in the Papua Highlands.

item.title: 'Woman who disguised herself as a man' wanted in connection with Monaco bombing
item.description: Investigators are hunting for a woman they say "disguised herself as a man" in connection with in a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy businessman in Monaco.

item.title: Daria Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon as Naomi Osaka stuns on and off the court
item.description: Daria Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon to end Australia's run in the women's draw.

item.title: Pokémon GO was a 2016 phenomenon. Ten years later, some players never stopped
item.description: One Pokémon GO fan walked 32,000 km in his search to "catch them all". On the way he found health, friendship and a partner.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: A July 4 special with an alien invasion, George III on TV and Taylor Swift
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Will Conroy's cat carcass distract from Labor's AUKUS conundrum?
item.description: After guiding the country through a tumultuous six months, the Albanese government faces tough fiscal and political decisions.

item.title: Australia has strengthened ties with Indonesia but our Asia capability is in decline
item.description: As Asian language enrolments wither Australia is failing to make lasting ties within our region.

item.title: As the US turns 250 this July 4, who is still proud to be an American?
item.description: Donald Trump has been pushing a patriotic message ahead of a July 4 milestone, but some Americans say they feel no national pride at this point in their country's history.

item.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic
item.description: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

item.title: What became of Jack?
item.description: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

item.title: How Avalon Emerson balances DJ stardom and indie pop
item.description: Avalon Emerson has built a global reputation as a DJ, but she soon found standing still was not for her.

item.title: Townsville boy left with broken eye socket from e-scooter collision
item.description: Cooper Blackshaw was not operating the e-scooter that injured him outside his school gates.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed, spokesperson confirms
item.description: A heavy media presence and barricades surrounded the Madison Square Garden stadium where fans waited in sweltering conditions.

item.title: Geological Survey of Victoria maps 200,000 unrecorded gold rush mining features
item.description: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.

item.title: Pub culture changing as young people 'get up at 5am, not home at 5am'
item.description: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.

item.title: Restaurant food scraps and horse manure key to soil farmers' success
item.description: While diners enjoy their seaside meals, Andrew Collins is happily outside chasing the scraps.

item.title: Extraordinary internal police emails revealed after Indigenous man allegedly beaten by officer
item.description: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

item.title: Osprey chicks hatch on Daintree live stream
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Osprey chicks hatch on Daintree crane nest live stream
item.description: A live stream running for more than a decade on top of an observatory crane in the World Heritage-listed Daintree Rainforest captures the hatching of osprey chicks.

item.title: Albanese government rejects all UN recommendations on improving LGBTQIA+ rights
item.description: The Albanese government has accepted the lowest number of recommendations made by the UN Human Rights Council in 20 years, with none protecting the LGBTQIA+ community.

item.title: Bird flu has arrived after months of algal bloom, but primary industries minister says SA is ready
item.description: While the arrival of bird flu on SA shores is a "huge concern", the state's primary industries minister maintains the government is prepared for the possibility of a wider local outbreak.

item.title: World Cup: Australia's story ends in heartbreak against Egypt, but Socceroos' path forward is now clear
item.description: The Socceroos exited the World Cup in the cruellest possible manner, on penalties to Egypt. But the roots of the defeat could be found in the 120 minutes of play, which clearly showed where Australia must improve.

item.title: Hip hop program You're Write helping Tasmanian kids find words for the feelings
item.description: A Tasmanian youth outreach program is providing free hip hop writing workshops for young people, which experts say could be "life-changing".

item.title: Moment Egypt clinched a World Cup win from the Socceroos
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mining giant Rio Tinto accused of abandoning NT town of Nhulunbuy to an uncertain future
item.description: In Australia's tropical north, a mining town is preparing for the departure of its biggest employer, and locals are accusing the mining giant of squeezing out small businesses on its way out.

item.title: NSW premier pledges $12 billion to revive train manufacturing in the Hunter
item.description: NSW Premier Chris Minns tells a conference of the party faithful that the state's next set of trains will be manufactured "where the original fleet was built".

item.title: Tiny forests popping up across Sydney as part of effort to restore urban tree canopy
item.description: Using enriched soil and densely planted trees, the pocket forests can fit into small underutilised spaces owned by councils.

item.title: Fans at Federation Square celebrate Socceroos' equalising goal against Egypt
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chilling Invasion Day image among this year's National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art Awards finalists
item.description: There is a wide range of artworks among the finalists vying for this year's prestigious National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art Awards, from photography and painting to multimedia and sculpture.

item.title: Memorial forest grown with cremated ashes opens in New South Wales first
item.description: When 10-year-old Tegan Chen died after accidentally slipping into a rushing creek on a walk, it stirred her parents to set up "living memorials" for others.

item.title: Taylor Swift marries Travis Kelce as fans celebrate America's 'royal wedding'
item.description: Pop superstar Taylor Swift and her partner Travis Kelce have married at a ceremony officiated by Adam Sandler.

item.title: Tracing Simon Carman and alleged murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla
item.description: Accused murderer Simon Peter Carman spent much of the past decade isolating himself from many of his friends and family in Australia. It's in his rented room in Thailand that local police allege a gruesome murder occurred.

item.title: Great Barrier Reef avoids 'in danger' listing in World Heritage Committee draft decision
item.description: The UN body raised "utmost concern" over the site's declining coral cover after mass bleaching events in 2024 and 2025.

item.title: Woman buys small Mingela pub to become one of Australia's youngest publicans
item.description: Kelsey Brown revives the tiny town of Mingela by buying the local watering hole that had been closed for more than a year.

item.title: Four rescued after 4WD swept into flooded Macalister River crossing in Gippsland
item.description: Four people are rescued from floodwaters after their 4WD is swept into a river crossing.

item.title: A cheesy, crunchy bake that will change your view of cabbage - ABC News
item.description: A one-pot meal that takes cabbage to another level with rich tomato sauce and a crispy, cheesy topping.

item.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, ABC explores what the word deadly means to the community
item.description: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

item.title: After 250 years, what now for the 'Great Democratic Experiment'?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How rugby league became Australia's most valuable football code
item.description: A $5 billion-plus broadcast rights deal is expected to be announced this week, making rugby league Australia's most valuable football code.

item.title: A look at the Declaration of Independence in the era of Donald Trump
item.description: The United States is celebrating its 250th anniversary today. Let's take a closer look at the document that birthed the nation and where things stand now.

item.title: Magpie Maynard makes contact with umpire after being hit 'cheaply'
item.description: Collingwood defender Brayden Maynard could be in trouble after making contact with an umpire during the Magpies' six-point win over the Gold Coast Suns.

item.title: How an AI catfish got Alicia's Instagram banned
item.description: Alicia discovered a Facebook account had gained more than 500,000 followers by posting AI-generated images of her.

item.title: Broncos cop eighth straight loss as Sharks get home at Lang Park
item.description: The battling Broncos lose their eighth straight game, going down 28-16 to the Sharks at Lang Park after the Dragons got just their second win of the season against the Tigers.

item.title: WA premier orders statue of former footballer Nicky Winmar to be removed after assault conviction
item.description: Former St Kilda and Western Bulldogs player Nicky Winmar has been found guilty of assaulting a woman in Victoria.

item.title: New biosecurity advice for egg producers as bird flu testing continues
item.description: States and territories are urged to encourage free-range producers to keep their birds indoors where practical in an effort to combat bird flu.

item.title: Thunderbirds win record-breaking Super Netball title
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds have unlocked a Super Netball dynasty with a unique coaching approach under Tania Obst. The three-time Super Netball champions defeated the Melbourne Vixens 61- 40 in the grand final at John Cain Arena.

item.title: Ukraine retaliates against Russia's deadliest attack on Kyiv, strikes St Petersburg
item.description: Ukraine has retaliated after Russia's attack on Kyiv killed at least 27 people.

item.title: Wallabies fall agonisingly short against Ireland in Nations Championship
item.description: Ben Donaldson is unable to land a winning penalty goal after the full-time siren, as the Wallabies go down 33-31 to Ireland in a pulsating Test.

item.title: Footballer in critical condition after sustaining head injury during match in Lalor
item.description: A 27-year-old player is in a critical condition after a serious mid-match injury during a football game in Lalor this afternoon.

item.title: NSW confirms first case of deadly bird flu strain H5N1 on Mid North Coast
item.description: The first case of the deadly H5 bird flu is confirmed in a giant petrel found on the NSW Mid North Coast.

item.title: De Minaur secures second week at Wimbledon with win against 'Aussie slayer'
item.description: World number 6 Alex de Minaur has won against the USA's Zach Svajda, a victory which will see him progress to the second week of Wimbledon.

item.title: Serena Williams pulls out of Wimbledon after 'tweaking' knee in Joint match
item.description: Serena Williams has pulled out of her Wimbledon doubles campaign alongside sister Venus, after sustaining a knee injury during her opening-round singles defeat to Australia's Maya Joint.

item.title: It's time for your weekly spelling check-up with Guy Montgomery
item.description: Will those primary school spelling sheets be enough to pull you a pass?

item.title: Australian influencer Lily Jay's tangled web of AI manipulation
item.description: Receiving millions of views online, the Lily Jay Foundation claims to help children across the world. She was even given an award for her work. But what is real and what is AI-generated? ABC NEWS Verify investigates.

item.title: We go behind the scenes to discover the secrets of Play School's 60-year success
item.description: Australian Story is given rare behind-the-scenes access to Play School as it celebrates 60 years on air. An intimate look at the creative process of the iconic children's TV show that's still guided by one golden rule.

item.title: Investors consider class action against super trustees after First Guardian and Shield collapses
item.description: Investors who lost out in a superannuation scam are considering a potential class action.

item.title: From George Washington to Donald Trump, America's creation story reads like a Hollywood script
item.description: US Independence Day was never the simple morality tale Americans often tell themselves. Under Donald Trump the debate over its meaning is more politically charged than ever.

item.title: Socceroos match shows where Labor's gambling ad laws fall short
item.description: There should be rules against jumping on the World Cup bandwagon to illustrate a political point, but the Socceroos' valiant effort against Egypt offers a timely real-world test.

item.title: Taylor Swift's 'royal wedding' was a lesson in media mastery
item.description: After mounting speculation, Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have officially tied the knot. One expert says what we know, and don't know, about the wedding is a lesson in "media mastery".

item.title: Surveillance for deadly H5 bird flu increased for reef's seabird population
item.description: A vulnerable colony of red-tailed tropicbirds is being monitored for the deadly H5 bird flu, with fears the impact on Australian native bird populations could be significant.

item.title: Wage increase the final straw for struggling small businesses
item.description: Small businesses struggling with rising costs say a recent wage increase is the final straw. In one case, the owner of a corner store that has been running for 70 years decided to close the business.

item.title: Before her alleged murder, Tunchanok Donhomla smiled through 'tough life'
item.description: While the public will remember Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla for the tragic nature of her death, her aunt, Mee Boonsert, will remember the baby she helped raise.

item.title: Wimbledon shock as Filipino Alexandra Eala makes history against Iga Świątek
item.description: Alexandra Eala is left in tears after her stunning victory over reigning Wimbledon champion Iga Świątek.

item.title: How Peter accidentally discovered the SS Gothenburg and 'all the bits of a great shipwreck story'
item.description: The startling noise of metal on metal was unusual on a coral reef and unlocked a nearly century-old mystery of a tragic maritime disaster, sunken treasure and a peculiar, inscribed turtle shell.

item.title: Dentists see complications after Australians travel overseas for treatments they cannot afford here
item.description: Janelle Jeal thought having dental care overseas would be life changing. It was, but for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Meet Mulga Bore Hard Rock, the Indigenous desert cousins keeping the spirit of AC/DC alive
item.description: Inspired by AC/DC and raised in a remote NT community, these young cousins have become one of Australia's most unlikely hard rock bands.

item.title: Back fracture sparks AFL national draft prospect Jobe Janeway's footy career
item.description: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

item.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration
item.description: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

item.title: Japan's LGBTQIA+ education plan ineffective and 'non-binding', experts say
item.description: The first national plan of its kind is broadly criticised by gay rights proponents and legal experts for supporting the status quo.

item.title: Greyhound injuries, deaths kept offline out of 'respect' for trainers and 'their animals', Tasracing says
item.description: If you watched greyhound race videos on Tasracing's website, you would likely never know that dozens of dogs were injured or killed during events last year.

item.title: Woman charged with murder after body of four-year-old child found in home on NSW Central Coast
item.description: The body of a four-year-old child has been found in a home in Wyong on the NSW Central Coast.

item.title: Man in critical condition after car fire at Scoresby in Melbourne's east
item.description: Police are investigating a car fire that left a man with life-threatening burns in Melbourne's east.

item.title: Prince Harry will not be joined by Meghan and two children on London visit
item.description: UK media report the decision was taken after a request for police protection was denied.

item.title: Fran Lebowitz doesn't have a smartphone
item.description: No-one emerges unscathed from Fran Lebowitz's quips, bon mots and scathing take-downs of New York's peculiarities and poor etiquette.

item.title: Nhulunbuy housing provider urged to get back to basics amid post-mining planning crunch
item.description: A government-funded housing provider in the Northern Territory mining town of Nhulunbuy has been criticised for "not doing enough" to support small business owners.

item.title: Mystery violin willed to Canberra Youth Orchestra has style dating to early 1700s, expert says
item.description: A violin gifted to not-for-profit organisation Music for Canberra bears the hallmarks of early 18th century European style. But one expert says only forensic testing can prove its true origins.

item.title: Student project sees high-altitude balloons reach 35 kilometres above Cobar
item.description: Students from Newcastle head to outback NSW to launch two balloons into the Earth's upper atmosphere.

item.title: Memorable stories from Jackie Chan filming First Strike in Falls Creek in the 1990s
item.description: Thirty years ago a Hollywood crew turned Falls Creek into Ukraine, and Jackie Chan nearly froze getting the shot.

item.title: Man dies after alleged assault at West End park in Brisbane
item.description: Police say a 66-year-old man has died in hospital after an "altercation" with another man in West End.

item.title: Australians cagey on Ellyse Perry injury ahead of women's T20 World Cup final
item.description: The women's T20 World Cup final looms in what could be Ellyse Perry's last match for Australia, and an injury cloud hangs over the champion.

item.title: World Cup: France finally breaks free of stubborn Paraguay on Independence Day
item.description: After strolling through the World Cup so far, France faced its first major test against the determined defence of Paraguay. In stifling heat and with tensions boiling over, Les Bleus came through largely unscathed.

item.title: Maintenance issues, outages plague WA standalone power systems
item.description: Farmers in WA's south say the standalone power systems they got from Western Power after being moved off the grid have been problematic.

item.title: Search continues for missing Dandenong woman last seen walking towards Parkdale Beach
item.description: Police found a car belonging to Yun, also known as Michelle, in a car park on Friday but no other signs of her whereabouts.

item.title: International experts say surveilling local birds, marine mammals a 'priority' in bird flu response
item.description: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

item.title: Banker-turned-barefoot mosaic artist and ceramicist spend six years recreating Klimt's The Kiss
item.description: When a bank manager discovered mosaics, he threw away his shoes and tie and vowed never to wear them again.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Mo Salah shows his class to Socceroos, Paraguay labelled a 'disgrace'
item.description: Mo Salah's classy post-penalty act to the stricken Socceroos and a Paraguay team blasted as a "disgrace". Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Liberal frontbencher Dan Tehan says coalition with One Nation not 'being talked about'
item.description: Shadow energy minister Dan Tehan says the Liberal party is not entertaining thoughts of forming a coalition with One Nation.

item.title: Geraldton band Ratsalad gains global renown with Jackass movie song
item.description: Geraldton band Ratsalad thought they were being scammed when the producer of the latest Jackass movie said they wanted to feature their song in the credits.

item.title: Love story: Finding love after loss, at the dog park
item.description: Jorgen and Jill Porse were in the depths of grief when they first met at their local dog park 15 years ago.

item.title: Researchers call for rare WA reptiles to be listed as endangered
item.description: New research has revealed the Pilbara barking gecko and Bungle Bungle robust slider are facing a heightened risk of extinction due to a rapidly warming climate.

item.title: Tasmania looks to introduce tougher laws for image-based abuse such as 'revenge porn' and deepfakes
item.description: Tasmania catches up with other jurisdictions with plans to make it an offence to share sexually explicit images including "revenge porn" and AI-generated deepfakes.

item.title: Father, daughter sail Newcastle to Norway returning yacht to its roots
item.description: A NSW family is returning a wooden yacht to its roots in Norway, but it has not been all smooth sailing.

item.title: For many football fans, this World Cup has been one of exclusion and travel bans
item.description: There's never been a more diverse World Cup in history, but the 2026 version has also been one of exclusion for many fans wanting to join in the fun.

item.title: Statue of former St Kilda player Nicky Winmar removed from Perth Stadium after assault conviction
item.description: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

item.title: NRL tries Hail Mary for Swift and Kelce after Knights end Dolphins' win streak
item.description: The NRL shoots its shot with the most famous couple in the world, suggesting Taylor Swift and NFL star Travis Kelce might want to attend next year's season openers in Las Vegas.

item.title: Trump commemorates 250th US Independence Day after storms force evacuation of National Mall
item.description: The occasion was complicated by severe storms that gathered near Washington, forcing event organisers to order an earlier evacuation.

item.title: Power surge home to down Roos, rulings handed down for Suns-Pies melee
item.description: Port Adelaide completes a hard-fought 21-point win over North Melbourne, St Kilda runs riot against Essendon, and the AFL fines rather than bans Brayden Maynard and Touk Miller for making contact with an umpire.

item.title: Magnitude-3.8 earthquake rocks coastal community along Victoria's Great Ocean Road
item.description: The magnitude-3.8 earthquake struck at a depth of 10 kilometres along Victoria's popular Great Ocean Road.

item.title: NSW Police investigating after man's body pulled from Murray River at Barooga
item.description: NSW Police are investigating the death of a man who was found in the Murray River.

item.title: Suspected space junk washes up on north Queensland beach
item.description: Authorities are trying to determine how to safely contain a growing cluster of mysterious space debris that keeps washing ashore on a north Queensland beach.

item.title: Two men charged after allegedly pointing fake gun at Sydney synagogue
item.description: Two men have been charged after allegedly pointing a fake gun at a Double Bay synagogue, six months after the Bondi Beach terrorist attack.

item.title: Epping footballer in end-of-life care after head clash during Melbourne game
item.description: Epping Football Netball Club's Nathan Fitzgerald, 27, is receiving end-of-life care after colliding with a teammate in a reserves game.

item.title: Property auction clearance rates pick up slightly as expert predicts slump to continue
item.description: Less than half of all properties that went to auction in Australia were actually sold over the last week.

item.title: Ebola treatment trial begins in Congo as outbreak continues to grow
item.description: A trial for an experimental Ebola treatment starts in Congo, where the largest-ever Ebola outbreak continues to grow.

item.title: Wildfires devastate forests in Europe as temperatures rise again
item.description: Firefighters have been battling forest infernos in heatwave-scarred Europe, as temperatures rise again.

item.title: Fears for Gaza doctor Hussam Abu Safiya detained in Israel
item.description: Hussam Abu Safiya's lawyers believe he is at risk of being killed behind bars in an Israeli prison.

item.title: Australia defeats England to win ICC Women's T20 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Australia has won the ICC Women's T20 World Cup in commanding style, defeating England by seven wickets in the final at Lord's.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 6: Who quit Pearl Jam last year and what do you know about Wimbledon?
item.description: Let us test your smarts about Captain James Cook's last voyage, the debut release of a popular British girl group and other historical events.

item.title: Race Around the World recap: Catch the week five films
item.description: From manga to life-sized dolls, catch up on the films from week five of Race Around the World.

item.title: The best way to break screen-time rules for your kids when you need to
item.description: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

item.title: The mystery of Australia's missing cash
item.description: There is no shortage of theories, from stacks of cash hidden at home to illicit activities, but little in the way of definitive answers.

item.title: The Venezuelan government is distracted by suspicion while its people desperately search for the missing
item.description: Instead of focusing all its efforts on coordinating a widescale rescue operation, the Venezuelan government has been accused of hindering the response, and as has become apparent, our ability to cover it.

item.title: The Imperial Presidency: How Donald Trump's second term is changing the White House forever
item.description: Donald Trump is undertaking regime change, not just in Venezuela, but on US soil and it is unlikely the presidency will ever be the same.

item.title: The economy is strong yet consumer sentiment is rock bottom
item.description: The paradox of a strong economy and stock market with very low consumer sentiment is not close to being resolved. Quite the opposite, the disconnect is, if anything, widening.

item.title: Gutsy England prevails over Mexico in instant World Cup classic
item.description: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through.

item.title: Record number of exchange traded funds hit share market, but investors warn of 'gambling' risk
item.description: A record number of exchange traded funds were added to the Australian share market in the past financial year.

item.title: AFP to push Australia's Pacific police training model to reduce UN costs
item.description: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.

item.title: ABC trials AI writing tools for news staff amid trust warnings
item.description: Experts and the journalists’ union have welcomed the ABC’s plans to use artificial intelligence responsibly, but warned misuse could damage trust in the public broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: How man behind Norway's viral Viking row turned team motivation into global sensation
item.description: Ole Frøystad has always been a passionate football fan and when Norway qualified for its first men's World Cup since 1998, he was inspired to create the iconic chant that's become a global sensation.

item.title: Domestic and family violence victim-survivors at risk of CTE, experts warn
item.description: Enduring brutal physical abuse can increase the risk of developing a sinister brain disease, yet many victim-survivors are unaware, experts warn.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese signs Ocean of Peace Alliance with Fiji
item.description: Australia and Fiji strike a new defence alliance in a major development that could reshape the Pacific's strategic landscape.

item.title: SA Police report to coroner into Somerton Man case complete 'very soon'
item.description: The man who worked out the identity of the Somerton Man through a DNA match says SA Police has told him it is close to finishing its report for the coroner to finally officially put the 78-year mystery to rest.

item.title: Hospital patients given new way to escalate care concerns in South Australia
item.description: After Ryan Bowman died at the Mount Gambier Hospital, his family said their concerns over his deteriorating health went unheard.

item.title: World Cup: US striker Folarin Balogun freed to play Belgium despite red card after Donald Trump call
item.description: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

item.title: ASX dragged down by banks and miners, Vault Minerals jumps on takeover battle — as it happened
item.description: The ASX closes lower, despite the Gulf ceasefire holding and a positive sentiment on global markets.

item.title: Mental health service supports new NT parents struggling with depression and anxiety
item.description: Natalya Wallace thought she just had the baby blues as a result of a traumatic birth experience. When she realised she needed mental health support, the young mother was relieved to find a new service in her rural area.

item.title: China to test ballistic missile in Pacific — as it happened
item.description: The Chinese government informed governments in the region about the test earlier today, the ABC has been told.

item.title: Heritage sheep breeder, 11, helping Hampshire Down variety avoid extinction
item.description: Mitch Dickens's passion for sheep genetics saw his parents give him his own stud, which they hope will help keep a heritage breed alive.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Naomi Osaka stuns Aryna Sabalenka to advance to quarterfinals
item.description: Naomi Osaka downs top seed Aryna Sabalenka in straight sets in a fourth round meeting of grand slam champions at Wimbledon.

item.title: Mount Isa barber leaves behind fresh fades and more on Mornington Island
item.description: Mount Isa barber Jon James travels to remote Mornington Island to teach barbering skills, hoping to leave behind a trade that will last long after he returns home.

item.title: Anthony Albanese 'unequivocally' apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue during podcast game
item.description: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

item.title: Safety fears spark calls for better protections for Neil the seal
item.description: The elephant seal has been attracting onlookers in southern Tasmania, as well as a growing social media audience, but fears for his wellbeing and concerns that some people aren't keeping their distance have led to calls to better protect Neil.

item.title: First Nations creators tell Indigenous stories through video games
item.description: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

item.title: Great Southern records highest rate of whooping cough cases in regional WA
item.description: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

item.title: Police to investigate whether cannibalism was involved in 4yo's death on Central Coast
item.description: Cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the NSW Central Coast, with an independent review to take place by the Department of Communities and Justice.

item.title: Wimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic beats Roger Federer record despite 'meltdowns'
item.description: Novak Djokovic fights off a plucky opponent and his own demons to etch his name into another corner of the record books at Wimbledon.

item.title: Lost teenagers rescued from Onkaparinga River National Park
item.description: Three teenagers were rescued by police after becoming lost in a national park in Adelaide's south.

item.title: With no electrician for 18 months, Boulia is enduring persistent blackouts
item.description: Residents in the remote town of Boulia sit waiting for their power to come back on and a solution to persistent power outages.

item.title: Nathan Fitzgerald's football club calls for investigation into cricket pitches on sports ovals
item.description: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

item.title: Developer quietly sells illegally cleared south Sydney site earmarked for now-rejected mansion
item.description: In 2020, Amir Abu Abara illegally cleared land in Barden Ridge to build a lavish mansion. After a fine, a replanting works order, and the rejection of his housing plan, he is no longer the site's owner.

item.title: Seven Hobart bars to close as owner goes into voluntary administration
item.description: Seven Hobart bars, including the Observatory Bar and the Republic, are closing "in the immediate term" as owner Pub Banc Group is placed into voluntary administration. The administrator says the aim is for the venues to reopen under new ownership.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar hits back at Jonas Vingegaard as Isaac del Toro claims Tour de France stage two
item.description: Tadej Pogačar and his UAE Team Emirates-XRG responded to Visma Lease a Bike's stage 1 victory with a superb one-two on stage 2 of the Tour de France.

item.title: A 'game of cat and mouse' in Gaza as Israel's Yellow Line moves further into territory
item.description: The "Yellow Line" that marks the boundary of Israeli-controlled territory in Gaza has been moved deeper into the enclave.

item.title: WA minister Paul Papalia quits politics, triggering Secret Harbour by-election
item.description: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

item.title: New cancer care program empowers Yarrabah residents undergoing treatment
item.description: A new program in Far North Queensland aims to make life easier for people receiving treatment for cancer, while reducing the number of people who develop the disease.

item.title: Meta policy director downplays claims policy change led to more antisemitic content
item.description: A Meta executive has downplayed claims a change in the company's policies led to a rise in antisemitism while giving evidence to the royal commission.

item.title: Humpback whale caught in fishing line
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Humpback whale freed from fishing gear on NSW Central Coast
item.description: Whale rescuers say there have been 24 entanglements already this migration season. Meanwhile, a small whale was euthanised after it stranded itself on a beach near Newcastle, despite rescue efforts from residents and experts.

item.title: Alleged Mongols bikie gang associate on the run after escaping custody during Darwin medical transfer
item.description: Police are searching for remand prisoner Isaac Thompson, 26, who they say is a "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw motorcycle gang, after he escaped custody during a medical transfer on Sunday night.

item.title: Nigel Farage referred to UK standards watchdog over undeclared gifts from crypto investor George Cottrell
item.description: Nigel Farage was reportedly provided with security services, social media support and accommodation by George Cottrell, a 32-year-old crypto investor who previously served eight months in prison for wire fraud.

item.title: Australia's Space agency says beach objects likely fuel pressure vessels from launch vehicle
item.description: Australia's Space Agency says objects found on a north Queensland beach are likely pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle.

item.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win H2 Grand Prix after disqualification heartbreak
item.description: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.

item.title: Maroons skipper Cameron Munster focused on State of Origin decider against Blues
item.description: Cameron Munster wants to honour Queensland State of Origin greats by leading the Maroons to a series victory over NSW in Brisbane on Wednesday night.

item.title: World Cup: Ice-cold Erling Haaland with the moments that matter to send Brazil crashing
item.description: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

item.title: Police search underway after 36yo man fatally stabbed at Taree
item.description: Police are asking for public help to find the person who fatally stabbed a man found at a home at Taree on the NSW Mid North Coast early this morning.

item.title: SANFL avoids strike action after months of negotiations over pay
item.description: SANFL Players' Association members have voted for a memorandum of understanding with the state league amid an ongoing decline in players' pay.

item.title: Rare humpback whale calf spotted off NSW coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rare humpback whale calf, believed to be a leucistic, spotted off NSW and Queensland coast
item.description: The calf, thought to be one of only a dozen in the Southern Hemisphere, has been spotted swimming alongside its mother.

item.title: Sophie Molineux praised after leading Australia to Women's T20 World Cup win
item.description: Sophie Molineux is praised for her leadership ability after guiding Australia to its Women's T20 World Cup victory.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Erling Haaland surprises himself
item.description: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

item.title: Socceroo Awer Mabil speaks on his team's multiculturalism
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Destabilising' Chinese ballistic missile test in Pacific prompts government concerns
item.description: China has test-fired a long-range missile with a dummy warhead in the Pacific Ocean, prompting concerns from the Australian government.

item.title: Queensland premier defends 'breach bail, go to jail' as concerns raised over capacity of youth detention centres
item.description: The Queensland premier has dismissed concerns about the capacity of youth detention centres to house juvenile offenders who are jailed for breaching their bail.

item.title: Four of our best self-saucing puddings for winter
item.description: You'd never guess it but self-saucing puddings are so easy to make. Here are four of our best for you try while the weather is cool.

item.title: Mabil says monoculture debate 'bulls***' as Socceroos return home from FIFA World Cup
item.description: The Socceroos star says anyone "trying to divide" the community is "probably not Australian themselves".

item.title: England's World Cup Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past
item.description: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

item.title: Comedian's 'terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change
item.description: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

item.title: This Place: Thaaklatjika Mingkana
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'Assassination plot' impeachment trial involving Duterte dynasty begins in Manila
item.description: A highly consequential impeachment trial against Philippines Vice-President Sara Duterte could spell the end for her political career and shape the nation's 2028 election.

item.title: Police investigating suspicious death of 17-year-old girl in remote NT community of Galiwin'ku
item.description: Northern Territory Police say the girl was found inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, and was declared dead at the scene. A 34-year-old man known to the woman has been arrested.

item.title: Court told 'Razor' scooter used to murder axe-wielding man near Newcastle
item.description: A Supreme Court jury hears a Razor-branded scooter was the alleged murder weapon used to kill a man who was armed with an axe during a violent brawl north of Newcastle.

item.title: 'Pink army' gathers in Rundle Mall to welcome home victorious Thunderbirds
item.description: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

item.title: High pressure system will remain over Tasmania
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New all-time Australian air pressure record under intense winter weather system
item.description: Preliminary data shows the highest ever pressure in Australia was recorded in Tasmania on Monday morning.

item.title: NSW man who disappeared from Perth beach 13 years ago likely killed by shark, inquest told
item.description: Martyn Tann was last seen at Mullaloo Beach in 2013, with a coroner telling an inquest into his disappearance that she believed he was dead, likely in a shark attack.

item.title: Edge Early Learning Gawler East closed for second time after child taken to hospital
item.description: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to close immediately for the second time this year.

item.title: Michael Still denies pushing for UOW executive role as ICAC probe continues
item.description: Just days after resigning as chancellor at the University of Wollongong (UOW), Michael Still faces the state's corruption watchdog.

item.title: AFP says young person formally warned over online threats made to Indian Prime Minister Modi
item.description: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

item.title: Hobart Deputy Lord Mayor Zelinda Sherlock under fire over taking office snacks
item.description: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

item.title: Men removed from NSW Labor State Conference demand answers
item.description: As Premier Chris Minns walked into the Sydney Town Hall, greeting delegates ahead of his speech, two men unfurled a Palestinian flag from the second-floor balcony before being escorted from the event by police.

item.title: Central Australian tourism funding injection celebrated after several challenging years
item.description: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

item.title: Vandals target West Australian Anzac landmark with antisemitic graffiti
item.description: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: ACT public school language education plan found 'not fit for purpose' by parliamentary committee
item.description: The ACT government's blueprint to deliver language education in Canberra public schools is "not fit for purpose", a parliamentary committee finds, prompting calls for the government to once again deliver a centralised ATAR-level language learning plan.

item.title: Fires rage in Kyiv after Russian missile, drone attack
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Second Russian attack on Kyiv in less than a week kills 18 on eve of NATO summit and Zelenskyy-Trump meeting
item.description: President Volodymyr ⁠Zelenskyy urges NATO to take "strong decisions" on boosting Ukraine's air defences following the strike.

item.title: Melbourne footballer Nathan Fitzgerald dies after suffering head injury during game
item.description: Footy player and teacher Nathan Fitzgerald has died after suffering critical head injuries during a reserves game in the city's north on the weekend.

item.title: China warns Australia over military alliance with Fiji which promises to 'act to meet the common danger'
item.description: China has warned Australia not to harm its interests in the Pacific after Anthony Albanese struck a landmark military alliance with Fiji.

item.title: Prince Harry and King Charles III in awkward stand-off over offer to stay at Buckingham Palace
item.description: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.
